methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Skyeton operates in the Defense & Security sector at the Growth stage. The comparable universe spans 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries. This peer set reflects the fragmented nature of frontier-tech defense businesses.

Peer-median valuation proved inapplicable due to insufficient clustering. Among the 20 Defense & Security peers identified, fewer than three companies aligned simultaneously on stage, valuation tier, and business model. A mechanistic sector-wide median would obscure material differences in technology maturity, customer concentration, and revenue scale across the peer set. Consequently, traditional comparable-company analysis cannot be reliably anchored without additional operational or financial metrics (revenue, EBITDA, customer contracts, or burn rate) specific to Skyeton.

Without triangulated valuation output, this analysis cannot responsibly propose a range. The core constraint is data sparsity: the Defense & Security peer universe lacks sufficient homogeneity at Skyeton's stage to support statistical inference. Future valuation work should prioritize collection of (1) Skyeton's revenue or ARR, (2) burn and runway, and (3) customer concentration metrics, which would enable benchmarking against stage-matched peers and support DCF or revenue-multiple approaches. Until such data is available, any valuation estimate would rest on speculation rather than comparable analysis.
